lens: security — judge whether an attacker can abuse the code via an unsafe input, an exposed secret, or an insecure default (untrusted input concatenated into a query/shell/path/template/redirect, a hard-coded or logged secret, a privileged action with no server-side authorization check, an insecure default such as TLS verification off / permissive CORS / verbose error leaks / wide permissions, a new path reaching something sensitive without authentication, new attack surface such as deserialization / upload / outbound fetch)
note: The labeled held-out set is one JSON object per line at held_out_path: {"input_code": <str>, "gold_band": [lo, hi]}. The harness (security.py), the spec scorer (security_spec.py), the judge scorer (judge/code-review/security/score.py), and the golden suite all read it verbatim. held_out_path matches judge/code-review/security/metric.json exactly. SWAP held_out.jsonl to plug in more labeled examples; ids are assigned by surviving-line order (sec-001, sec-002, ...) when a line omits an explicit id.
